req.title: Rate limiting (boundary variant)
req.statement: The API shall enforce 60 requests per minute per user; overages shall return 429 with retry-after. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-049 mandates a hard cap of 60 requests/minute/user with 429+Retry-After on overage. Four tests are designed: (1) EP-positive: 30 requests (well within quota) all return non-429; (2) NEG-negative: 61 requests fired rapidly, verifying the 61st returns 429 with Retry-After header; (3) BVA-boundary-lower: exactly 60 requests return success (at-limit, not over); (4) BVA-boundary-upper: request 61 immediately after 60 returns 429 and Retry-After is a positive integer. Each test uses a fresh authenticated user token via <auth_fixture> and calls a lightweight read endpoint to minimise side-effects. Window reset is enforced by <reset_rate_limit> helper before each test to ensure idempotency.

tests.name: test_requests_well_within_limit_all_succeed
preconditions: Rate-limit window is fresh (no prior requests in current minute)
actions: Reset rate-limit window for the test user via <reset_rate_limit>
Send 30 GET requests to /api/v1/accounts with valid Authorization header
Collect all HTTP response status codes
expected: No Retry-After header present on any response
trace: R-FIN-049: requests below threshold must not be rate-limited
EP: mid-partition value (30) is representative of the allowed range [1,60]
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ENDPOINT = "/api/v1/accounts"


def test_requests_well_within_limit_all_succeed(auth_fixture, reset_rate_limit):
    token = auth_fixture
    reset_rate_limit(token)
    statuses = []
    for _ in range(30):
        r = requests.get(
            BASE_URL + ENDPOINT,
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
            timeout=5,
        )
        statuses.append(r.status_code)
    assert all(s != 429 for s in statuses), (
        f"Unexpected 429 within quota. Statuses: {statuses}"
    )
    assert len(statuses) == 30

tests.name: test_sixty_first_request_returns_429_with_retry_after
actions: Reset rate-limit window for the test user via <reset_rate_limit>
Send 60 GET requests to consume the full quota
Inspect status code and Retry-After header of the 61st response
expected: Retry-After header is present on the 61st response
Retry-After value is a positive integer (<= 60)
trace: R-FIN-049: overages shall return 429 with retry-after
NEG: deliberately exceed the allowed quota to trigger the error pathway
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ENDPOINT = "/api/v1/accounts"


def test_sixty_first_request_returns_429_with_retry_after(auth_fixture, reset_rate_limit):
    token = auth_fixture
    reset_rate_limit(token)
    hdrs = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    for _ in range(60):
        requests.get(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=hdrs, timeout=5)
    r = requests.get(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=hdrs, timeout=5)
    assert r.status_code == 429, f"Expected 429, got {r.status_code}"
    assert "Retry-After" in r.headers, "Retry-After header missing on 429 response"
    retry_after = int(r.headers["Retry-After"])
    assert 1 <= retry_after <= 60, f"Retry-After out of expected range: {retry_after}"

tests.name: test_exactly_sixty_requests_at_boundary_all_succeed
actions: Reset rate-limit window for the test user via <reset_rate_limit>
Record status of every response, especially the 60th
expected: 60th response is specifically not 429 (at-limit boundary is inclusive)
trace: R-FIN-049: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold must be tested
BVA: tests the upper inclusive boundary value of the allowed partition (N=60)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ENDPOINT = "/api/v1/accounts"


def test_exactly_sixty_requests_at_boundary_all_succeed(auth_fixture, reset_rate_limit):
    token = auth_fixture
    reset_rate_limit(token)
    hdrs = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    statuses = []
    for _ in range(60):
        r = requests.get(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=hdrs, timeout=5)
        statuses.append(r.status_code)
    assert len(statuses) == 60
    assert statuses[-1] != 429, (
        f"60th request (boundary) must not be rate-limited, got {statuses[-1]}"
    )
    assert all(s != 429 for s in statuses), (
        f"Unexpected 429 within quota. Statuses: {statuses}"
    )

tests.name: test_rate_limit_window_resets_after_one_minute
preconditions: Test environment permits a ~65-second wall-clock wait
actions: Send 61 requests to trigger 429 on the last
Wait 65 seconds for the rate-limit window to expire
Send one additional request after window reset
expected: Request sent after window expiry returns status != 429
System has transitioned from rate-limited state back to allowed state
trace: R-FIN-049: per-minute window implies state transition from limited back to normal
tests.executable:
import time
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ENDPOINT = "/api/v1/accounts"
WINDOW_WAIT = 65


@pytest.mark.slow
def test_rate_limit_window_resets_after_one_minute(auth_fixture, reset_rate_limit):
    token = auth_fixture
    reset_rate_limit(token)
    hdrs = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    for _ in range(60):
        requests.get(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=hdrs, timeout=5)
    over = requests.get(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=hdrs, timeout=5)
    assert over.status_code == 429, f"Expected 429 on request 61, got {over.status_code}"
    time.sleep(WINDOW_WAIT)
    after_reset = requests.get(BASE_URL + ENDPOINT, headers=hdrs, timeout=5)
    assert after_reset.status_code != 429, (
        f"Expected non-429 after window reset, got {after_reset.status_code}"
    )
